title: Controlled Vocabulary: Archetype Graph Node Roles
description: Canonical set of node roles used across all 15 archetype graphs. Every node must be assigned exactly one role. Shared roles enable the cross-archetype index and support comparative analysis.

node_roles.definition: The character's initial stable state before the story's central tension begins.
node_roles.structural_function: Establishes baseline for measuring change. Always a start node.
node_roles.examples_across_archetypes: Ordinary World (Hero's Journey), Disadvantaged State (Rags to Riches), Pre-Quest Life (The Quest)

node_roles.definition: An event or realization that breaks the existing equilibrium and creates the central problem.
node_roles.structural_function: Launches the story's engine. Typically follows Origin.
node_roles.examples_across_archetypes: Call to Adventure (Hero's Journey), Inciting Crime (The Revenge), Discovery of Confinement (The Escape)

node_roles.definition: A point of no return where the character commits to engaging with the central problem.
node_roles.structural_function: Separates setup from the story's main action. Marks irreversible entry.
node_roles.examples_across_archetypes: Crossing the Threshold (Hero's Journey), Accepting the Quest (The Quest), Entering the Unfamiliar World (Voyage and Return)

node_roles.definition: A challenge that tests the character's abilities, resolve, or values.
node_roles.structural_function: Builds progressive difficulty. Often appears in sequences or loops.
node_roles.examples_across_archetypes: Road of Trials (Hero's Journey), Escalating Obstacles (The Quest), Confronting Social Barriers (Rags to Riches)

node_roles.definition: A moment where critical hidden information is exposed, changing understanding.
node_roles.structural_function: Recontextualizes prior events. Forces reassessment of strategy or identity.
node_roles.examples_across_archetypes: Clue Breakthrough (Mystery Unveiled), True Nature of Monster (Overcoming the Monster), Hidden Cost Revealed (The Sacrifice)

node_roles.definition: A turning point where fortune, power, or trajectory shifts dramatically.
node_roles.structural_function: Creates pivots in the story arc. Can be positive or negative.
node_roles.examples_across_archetypes: Fall from Grace (Rise and Fall), Reversal of Fortune (Tragedy), Tables Turned (The Revenge)

node_roles.definition: A deliberate choice where the character doubles down despite full knowledge of the cost.
node_roles.structural_function: Distinguishes passive experience from active agency. Raises moral weight.
node_roles.examples_across_archetypes: Ordeal Decision (Hero's Journey), Choosing Revenge over Peace (The Revenge), Accepting the Sacrifice (The Sacrifice)

node_roles.definition: The moment of maximum threat or tension where the central conflict reaches its peak.
node_roles.structural_function: The structural climax. All prior nodes converge here.
node_roles.examples_across_archetypes: Supreme Ordeal (Hero's Journey), Final Confrontation (Overcoming the Monster), Point of No Return (Tragedy)

node_roles.definition: A fundamental change in the character's identity, worldview, or capability.
node_roles.structural_function: Marks the story's core payoff — the character is no longer who they were.
node_roles.examples_across_archetypes: Apotheosis (Hero's Journey), Moral Renewal (Rebirth), Identity Shift (The Transformation)

node_roles.definition: A permanent loss that cannot be undone, giving weight to the story's outcome.
node_roles.structural_function: Prevents cheap resolution. Ensures consequences are real.
node_roles.examples_across_archetypes: Sacrifice Made (The Sacrifice), Tragic Death (Tragedy), Freedom's Price (The Escape)

node_roles.definition: The new stable state after the central conflict is resolved.
node_roles.structural_function: Terminal node. Shows the lasting impact of the journey.
node_roles.examples_across_archetypes: Return with Elixir (Hero's Journey), New Status (Rags to Riches), Order Restored (Comedy)

node_roles.definition: A movement into a darker, more dangerous, or more constrained state.
node_roles.structural_function: Increases pressure and narrows options. Often precedes Crisis.
node_roles.examples_across_archetypes: Descent into the Underworld (Hero's Journey), Moral Corruption (Rise and Fall), Deepening Entrapment (The Escape)

node_roles.definition: An external event or character that accelerates or redirects the story's trajectory.
node_roles.structural_function: Provides momentum at points where the story might stall.
node_roles.examples_across_archetypes: Mentor Appearance (Hero's Journey), Key Clue (Mystery Unveiled), Ally or Betrayer (The Quest)

node_roles.definition: A moment of moral or practical accounting where the character faces the consequences of prior choices.
node_roles.structural_function: Links cause to effect. Often immediately precedes or follows Crisis.
node_roles.examples_across_archetypes: Tragic Downfall (Tragedy), Revenge Fulfilled (The Revenge), Judgment (Rise and Fall)
node_roles._note: Assigned to RV_N07_FINAL_RECKONING in The Revenge archetype. Available for future use in other archetypes or hybrid modeling.
